targetSelectionNote: Stage review marks toolSelection as `ready` with high measurement confidence. The weakest overall dimension (execution, 3.33) is explicitly flagged as a downstream, noisy symptom of toolSelection failures plus a calibration artifact. Synthesis and synthesisBudget are also downstream/noisy. toolSelection at 3.67 < 4.0 automation floor with a stable, reproducible failure cluster is the correct Layer 3 target.
rootCause: In context/lib/ai/query-clarification.ts, the partial-clause-coverage branch gated by preserveNamedTargetClarification (the strict named-target planning guard) unconditionally returns triggerClarification: true with decisionReasonCode: 'contract_scope_ambiguity' whenever (a) the query matches the quoted-literal regex, (b) deterministic target resolution is required, and (c) at least one clause is unsupported. It performs that branch even when the surviving viable/partial-coverage candidate set collapses to a single grounded method whose schema required params are already fully bound and whose semantic profile is querySafe. Result: six answerable named-target prompts exit with zero grounded tool calls despite the clarification menu already listing exactly one executable recommendation. The guard is correct in principle but has no escape hatch for the materially-one-grounded-method case.
problemStatement: When a named-target query reaches the strict named-target planning guard and the partial-clause-coverage branch determines that only one grounded method is materially viable AND its schema required params are already resolved, the runtime should bind and execute that method instead of emitting clarification_required.
runtimeInvariant: If the deterministic named-target analysis leaves exactly one materially-distinct grounded method that is queryEligible, queryReadOnlySafe, semanticProfile.querySafe, and has no unresolvedRequiredParams, that method is a single grounded interpretation by contract definition and must not be blocked on clarification regardless of clause-coverage partiality. Clarification is only warranted when two or more materially-distinct grounded methods remain.

proposedExperiment.summary: Add a narrowly scoped, schema-driven escape hatch inside the strict named-target planning guard's partial-clause-coverage branch that falls through to the existing single_grounded_interpretation return path when the partial-coverage clarification set collapses to a single resolvable grounded method. Gate behind a new rollout sub-flag.
designNotes: In classifyClarificationNeed (query-clarification.ts), before the existing `if (preserveNamedTargetClarification) { return triggerClarification: true ... }` block, compute singleResolvedGroundedMethod from contract signals only: partialCoverageClarificationCandidates.length <= 1 OR every surviving candidate shares the same (toolId, methodName); AND partialCoverageRecommended.unresolvedRequiredParams.length === 0; AND partialCoverageRecommended.meta.queryEligible && queryReadOnlySafe && semanticProfile.querySafe; AND partialCoverageRecommended is identical to viableCandidates[0].

When singleResolvedGroundedMethod is true AND the new namedTargetGuardAutoBindOnResolved rollout sub-flag is 'on', return the existing shape: triggerClarification: false, decisionReasonCode: 'single_grounded_interpretation', decisionSignals: ['named_target_guard_auto_bind_on_resolved','partial_clause_coverage'], with the same recommendedCandidate.

Add namedTargetGuardAutoBindOnResolved: 'off'|'on' to DualSurfaceRolloutFlags, parsed from env DUAL_SURFACE_NAMED_TARGET_GUARD_AUTOBIND_ON_RESOLVED. Default 'off'. Export shouldAutoBindSingleResolvedNamedTarget(flags).
Emit logDualSurfaceRolloutTelemetry('named-target-guard-auto-bind', { reasonCode, toolId, methodName }) so the regression harness can count fires per contributor.

Keep the strict named-target planning guard fully active for all other cases: 2+ materially-distinct grounded methods, unresolved required params, non-querySafe semantic profiles, cross-tool ambiguity.

rolloutFlagIfAny.name: namedTargetGuardAutoBindOnResolved
rolloutFlagIfAny.killSwitch: Unset env var or set to 'off' to fully restore prior clarification behavior.

proposedExperiment.contributorAgnosticRationale: All gating signals are derived from the shared ClarificationCandidate contract: toolId, methodName, unresolvedRequiredParams, meta.queryEligible, queryReadOnlySafe, semanticProfile.querySafe. None reference vendor, tool, or venue names, market slugs, or prompt wording.

cases: regression: named-target query with ONE viable grounded candidate whose unresolvedRequiredParams is empty and semanticProfile.querySafe is true -> triggerClarification:false and decisionReasonCode:'single_grounded_interpretation' when the flag is 'on'. Flag 'off' preserves today's contract_scope_ambiguity clarification.
generalization: synthetic vendor-agnostic named-target fixture with the same shape produces the same auto-bind outcome; must not reference Polymarket/Kalshi/any real tool name.
ambiguity preserved: two materially-distinct grounded candidates from the SAME tool with an unsupported clause still clarify even with the flag 'on'.
unresolved schema: single grounded candidate but unresolvedRequiredParams non-empty still clarifies with the flag 'on'.
capability-miss preserved: no queryEligible candidates -> still capability_miss regardless of flag.
cross-tool ambiguity preserved: two candidates from DIFFERENT toolIds -> still clarifies with flag 'on'.

dimensionScoresAfterHypothesis.toolSelection: >= 4.4 (6 traces flip from zero-grounded-calls to grounded answers)
dimensionScoresAfterHypothesis.clarification: >= 4.3 (paired; gate no longer misfires on materially-single grounded method)

regressionGuardrails.overfittingWarning: Reject if only polymarket improves while kalshi regresses or stays flat.

riskOfRegression.primary: Auto-bind could fire when unresolvedRequiredParams is empty only because the schema is under-specified, causing a weakly-bound call. Mitigated by also requiring semanticProfile.querySafe and by preserving the full guard for any multi-candidate case.
riskOfRegression.secondary: Synthesis could inherit partially-covered answers when some clauses have no grounded method. Mitigated because the non-guard branch today already treats this as single_grounded_interpretation; we are only aligning guard-on with guard-off behavior.
riskOfRegression.tertiary: Leaving the sub-flag 'on' by default in production could silently relax the guard on future contributors. Mitigated by default 'off' + explicit opt-in from the experiment runner.

riskChecklist: Do not relax the guard for multi-tool candidate sets.
Do not relax the guard when unresolvedRequiredParams is non-empty.
Do not collapse capability_miss vs clarification_required vs answer categories.
Do not bypass semanticProfile.querySafe or queryReadOnlySafe.
Do not modify any contributor server.ts.
Do not change benchmark thresholds or scoring.

marketplaceRobustnessCheck.generalization: Keys on shared-schema invariants (unresolvedRequiredParams, semanticProfile.querySafe, queryEligible, queryReadOnlySafe, toolId/methodName identity). Future contributors with rich schemas benefit automatically; those with under-specified schemas retain stricter clarification.
marketplaceRobustnessCheck.futureContributorThoughtExperiment: Contributor A with get_quote_snapshot(requiredParams:['symbol']) and a quoted-symbol query auto-binds as intended. Contributor B with the same method name but no requiredParams metadata or no querySafe profile will NOT auto-bind and will keep clarifying. Contributor C whose query mentions two materially-distinct grounded methods still clarifies.
marketplaceRobustnessCheck.codePathParity: All changes live in context/lib/ai/ and context/lib/rollout/, shared by /api/query and /api/chat. Both surfaces inherit the fix.
marketplaceRobustnessCheck.docsAlignment: No user-visible contract in @context-docs/ changes; we are aligning the guard with the already-documented single_grounded_interpretation semantics.

targetedChecks: Re-run runtime-stage-audit on polymarket-contributor: q2, q8, q9, q10, q12, q17 each produce >=1 grounded tool call and a grounded synthesis outcome.
contract_scope_ambiguity count across the 18-trace set drops by >=5.
No new capability_miss outcomes on previously-grounded traces.
regressionGates: no increase in capability_miss on either contributor

rejectionCriteria: polymarket queryMean drops below 4.77 after the change.
kalshi queryMean drops below 4.37 after the change.
Any of q2, q8, q9, q10, q12, q17 still exits with zero grounded tool calls.
Auto-bind telemetry fires on a case that has 2+ materially-distinct grounded candidates.
Any previously-clarified trace silently becomes capability_miss instead of clarification.
Code change touches a contributor server.ts or introduces a tool/venue-name string match.
The generalization test referencing a synthetic vendor-agnostic fixture fails.
rejectionEscalation: If two closely-related experiments targeting this same deterministic-resolution path are rejected, escalate to the orchestrator as a potential architectural blocker in the strict named-target planning guard rather than iterating again on the same cluster.
